Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `last_animation`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
`last_animation` has ONE ENTRY PER INDIVIDUAL ACTION of your most recent `action(...)` call, in execution order; each entry's `.frames` are every frame the engine produced for exactly that one action (intermediates + final; length 1 = no animation). After action(['UP','UP','LEFT']) there are three entries -- first UP, second UP, then LEFT. All change within one entry's frames is that single action animating, never several actions. Step through entries to read per-move motion and causality.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: UP moves the player vertically along the left edge. RIGHT extends the path horizontally. The player moved up one row. Need to figure out how to enter the room and reach the colored targets.
- Plan: First inspect the board segmentation to understand exact positions and relationships, then navigate to collect items in the correct order.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the player is still at the same position - RIGHT just extended the path again. The player is at row ~30-35 now (moved up from original ~36-41). The path tiles extend horizontally.
So the controls seem to be:
- UP/DOWN: Move player vertically along the left edge
- RIGHT: Extend the path horizontally
- LEFT: Probably retract the path or move player left
But how do I enter the room? Maybe I need to position the player at the right height (aligned with the room entrance) and then press RIGHT to enter? Or maybe I need to use MOUSE or ACTION7?
Let me look at the room entrance more carefully. The room boundary showed an opening on the left side. The player needs to be aligned with that opening.
Looking at the initial data:
- Room boundary: [[12, 17], [12, 46], [41, 46], [41, 17], [40, 17], [40, 22], [39, 23], [38, 23], [37, 22], [37, 17]]
- The opening seems to be around rows 37-40, cols 17-23
The player was originally at rows 36-41, which aligns with the opening. But moving RIGHT from there just extended the path.
Wait - maybe this game doesn't involve the player physically entering the room. Maybe the path IS the player's reach, and I need to use MOUSE to click on targets, or use ACTION7 for something.
Let me try MOUSE to click on one of the colored targets, or try ACTION7.
Actually, let me reconsider. The path tiles extend from the player into the room. The targets are on the right wall. Maybe I need to:
1. Position the player at the right height (using UP/DOWN)
2. Extend the path to reach the target (using RIGHT)
3. Use some action to "collect" or "interact" with the target
Let me try positioning the player at the red target's height and extending the path, then trying ACTION or MOUSE.
The red target is at rows 19-22. Let me move the player up to align with it.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The path already extends all the way to the right wall (col 46). The targets are at col 42-45. So the path already reaches them horizontally.
Maybe I need to align the player vertically with a target and then use some action to collect it? Let me try moving the player to align with the red target (row 19-22) and then try ACTION7 or MOUSE.
But first, let me understand: the player is currently at row 30-35. The red target is at row 19-22. I need to move up about 8-10 more rows.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the path seems to be at a specific row (around row 38-39 based on earlier data). The player moves up/down but the path stays at its row. So maybe the path is independent of player vertical position?
Let me try a different approach: use MOUSE to click on the red target directly.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: UP/DOWN moves the player vertically along the left edge. RIGHT extends the path horizontally (already reaches the right wall). The player cannot enter the room by movement alone. Need to try MOUSE or ACTION7 to interact with targets. The bottom panel shows collection order: Pink→Red→Green→Blue.

# Red target is at boundary [[19, 42], [19, 45], [22, 45], [22, 42]]
# Center would be around row 20-21, col 43-44
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 43}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
